summary: Package fetches and eval()s a third-party GitHub Gist on require()
details: On module load, index.js performs an HTTPS fetch against api.github.com for a Gist owned by user `getchainverse` (https://gist.github.com/getchainverse/b57a92378ad0a52430137c3b810e7107.js), extracts the content of the first file in the Gist response, and passes it directly to eval(). The package is published under a different author (`guilderguzman`), so the executed bytes are controlled by an unrelated third party and can be mutated at any time without republishing the package. The Gist content is unpinned, unauthenticated against any hash, and bears no relationship to the package's stated purpose ("simple node for sql fetch"). Any process that requires this package executes whatever JavaScript that Gist currently returns, giving the Gist owner remote code execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
